Project.Brief history of your project and organi:
The exaSPIM directory in the public datasets managed by the Allen Institute for Neural Dynamics (AIND) is a root directory specifically for storing imaging data generated by Expansion-assisted Selective Plane Illumination Microscopy. It is primarily used to host high-resolution 3D volumetric images of centimeter-scale tissues (such as whole mouse brains) after expansion and clearing, including raw images, metadata, processing pipeline outputs, OME-Zarr files, and other formatted data, along with supporting data processing and analysis toolchains.

• Technical Core: Enables nanoscale-resolution imaging of centimeter-scale samples (e.g., ~375 nm lateral and ~750 nm axial resolution after 4× expansion), allowing intact whole-mouse-brain imaging without sectioning.

• Data Content: Multi-channel, multi-view, large-field-of-view 3D volumetric data (single datasets can reach hundreds of terabytes), organized into subfolders by experiment ID/timestamp.
Project.Describe the data being stored onto File:
The exaSPIM directory in the public datasets managed by the Allen Institute for Neural Dynamics (AIND) is a root directory specifically for storing imaging data generated by Expansion-assisted Selective Plane Illumination Microscopy. It is primarily used to host high-resolution 3D volumetric images of centimeter-scale tissues (such as whole mouse brains) after expansion and clearing, including raw images, metadata, processing pipeline outputs, OME-Zarr files, and other formatted data, along with supporting data processing and analysis toolchains.

• Technical Core: Enables nanoscale-resolution imaging of centimeter-scale samples (e.g., ~375 nm lateral and ~750 nm axial resolution after 4× expansion), allowing intact whole-mouse-brain imaging without sectioning.

• Data Content: Multi-channel, multi-view, large-field-of-view 3D volumetric data (single datasets can reach hundreds of terabytes), organized into subfolders by experiment ID/timestamp.
Project.How do you plan to prepare the dataset: We use the AWS CLI to download data from AWS, cut it into appropriate sizes, and package it into CAR files. We then send them to the SPs via HTTP or email. We also use our own DC distributor to distribute DCs to the SPs. This allows us to effectively control the number of CAR files distributed and the proportion of DCs allocated in each round, ensuring fairness.
